ogTitle: Myuna Bay vs Nabiac: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Myuna Bay and Nabiac.
heading: Myuna Bay vs Nabiac — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Myuna Bay, New South Wales and Nabiac, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Myuna Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Nabiac (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Myuna Bay or Nabiac safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Myuna Bay has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Nabiac scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Myuna Bay scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Nabiac scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Myuna Bay or Nabiac?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Myuna Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
